name: FlatBuffers (Zero-Copy Binary Serialization)

performance.notes: Zero-copy reads — no parsing/deserialization required; fastest binary format for read-heavy workloads

agent_readiness.notes: Zero-copy binary serialization by Google. Define schema in .fbs file: table Monster { hp:short; name:string; }. flatc --python monster.fbs generates Python code. import flatbuffers. builder = flatbuffers.Builder(256). name = builder.CreateString('Orc'). MonsterStart(builder). MonsterAddHp(builder, 300). MonsterAddName(builder, name). monster = MonsterEnd(builder). builder.Finish(monster). buf = builder.Output(). Read: monster = Monster.Monster.GetRootAs(buf, 0). monster.Hp(). Used by TensorFlow Lite, ML Kit. Faster than protobuf for read-heavy. Schema evolution supported.

gotchas: Builder API is verbose and imperative — much harder to use than protobuf or msgpack
Schema file compilation step required (flatc compiler)
Python API is significantly less ergonomic than C++ or Rust bindings
Write performance is worse than read — building is complex
Little Python ecosystem support compared to protobuf
best_when: Read-heavy workloads where zero-copy deserialization is critical
TensorFlow Lite model packaging (uses FlatBuffers natively)
Game networking with very low latency requirements
avoid_when: General serialization needs (use msgpack or protobuf instead)
Python-centric workflows (ergonomics are poor in Python)
When protobuf schema evolution features are needed
summary: FlatBuffers provides zero-copy binary serialization — read without parsing/deserialization. Schema-defined (.fbs) with flatc compiler. Builder pattern for construction (verbose). Zero allocation reads via direct buffer access. Used by TensorFlow Lite. Faster than protobuf for read-heavy workloads. Apache-2.0 by Google. Python ergonomics are poor — prefer msgpack or protobuf for Python-first projects. Use when zero-copy read performance is critical.
